definition: Change In State of Delivery — a pre-CHoCH signal where price shifts from consistently delivering bearish candles to bullish ones (or vice versa). The LAST candle of the old delivery state becomes the entry zone.
depends_on: market_structure (CISD is a refinement of CHoCH detection)
displacement (CISD is confirmed when displacement follows the state change)
feeds_into: order_blocks (the CISD candle IS an order block)
trade entry (CISD provides earlier entry than waiting for full CHoCH)
related_to: PO3_AMD (CISD marks the transition from manipulation to distribution)
vs_CHoCH: CHoCH requires a swing point to be broken. CISD fires BEFORE that — it detects the shift in candle delivery. CISD is the early warning; CHoCH is the confirmation.
how_to_trade: When candle delivery shifts (3+ bearish candles followed by 1 bullish candle with body close above prior candle high), the bullish candle's low is the entry zone. Enter on retest. SL below the manipulation extreme.
PO3_connection: CISD typically occurs at the exact moment PO3 transitions from manipulation to distribution. It IS the phase transition signal.
